title: Lilnas Code Review Findings
description: Structured output schema for lilnas-code-review reviewer sub-agents. Compact-return only — no artifact file writes.

reviewer.description: Persona name that produced this output (e.g., 'correctness', 'security', 'kieran-typescript')

findings.description: List of code review findings. Empty array if no issues found.

title.description: Short, specific issue title. 10 words or fewer.

file.description: Relative file path from repository root

line.description: Primary line number of the issue

why_it_matters.description: Impact and failure mode — not 'what is wrong' but 'what breaks'. Lead with observable behavior. 2-4 sentences.

problem_code.description: The exact code snippet (10-20 lines) from the diff that demonstrates the issue. Include enough context to be self-contained.

suggested_fix.description: Concrete minimal fix snippet showing the recommended replacement. Omit or null if no good fix is obvious — a bad suggestion is worse than none.

fix_options.description: Optional array of alternative fix approaches when multiple reasonable options exist. Each item describes one option with pros/cons.

label.description: Short name for this option (e.g., 'Option A — extract helper')

tradeoffs.description: One-line summary of pros and cons

confidence.description: Anchored confidence score. Use exactly one of 0, 25, 50, 75, 100. Each anchor has a behavioral criterion the reviewer must honestly self-apply. 0: Not confident. False positive. 25: Somewhat confident. Could not verify. 50: Moderately confident. Verified real but minor/stylistic. 75: Highly confident. Will affect users/runtime in normal usage. 100: Absolutely certain. Verifiable from code alone.

evidence.description: Code-grounded evidence: snippets, line references, or pattern descriptions. At least 1 item.

pre_existing.description: True if this issue exists in unchanged code unrelated to the current diff

lens.description: Free-form label naming the lens/persona that surfaced this finding (e.g., 'correctness', 'kieran-typescript', 'security').

residual_risks.description: Risks the reviewer noticed but could not confirm as findings

testing_gaps.description: Missing test coverage the reviewer identified

lilnas_simplifications.description: lilnas-code-review trims ce-code-review's full schema in two ways: (1) compact-return only — no artifact file writes — so 'why_it_matters' and 'evidence' are required directly in the return; (2) autofix_class, owner, and requires_verification are dropped because lilnas-code-review does not apply fixes or route owners. The orchestrator uses severity + confidence + lens for sorting and rendering.

confidence_anchors.description: Confidence is one of 5 discrete anchors (0, 25, 50, 75, 100), each tied to a behavioral criterion the reviewer can honestly self-apply. Float values (e.g., 0.73) are not valid — the model cannot meaningfully calibrate at finer granularity, and discrete anchors prevent false-precision gaming.
confidence_anchors.0: False positive or pre-existing — do not report
confidence_anchors.25: Speculative; could not verify — do not report
confidence_anchors.50: Verified real but minor or stylistic — report only when P0
confidence_anchors.75: Highly confident, will affect users or runtime in normal usage — report
confidence_anchors.100: Verifiable from code alone (compile error, type mismatch, definitive logic bug, quoted standards violation) — report

confidence_thresholds.suppress: Below anchor 75 — do not report. Exception: P0 findings at anchor 50+ may be reported (critical-but-uncertain issues must not be silently dropped).
confidence_thresholds.report: Anchor 75 or 100 — include with full evidence.

severity_definitions.P0: Critical breakage, exploitable vulnerability, data loss/corruption. Must fix before merge.
severity_definitions.P1: High-impact defect likely hit in normal usage, breaking contract. Should fix.
severity_definitions.P2: Moderate issue with meaningful downside (edge case, perf regression, maintainability trap). Fix if straightforward.
severity_definitions.P3: Low-impact, narrow scope, minor improvement. User's discretion.
